description_long: This object is a rugged, military-style radio transceiver unit, featuring a rectangular, olive-green metal housing with a prominent black heat sink at one end. The front panel is equipped with a digital display, a numeric keypad, and several physical buttons and rotary knobs for tuning and control, as well as multiple indicator lights. A coiled cord connects to a classic handset, which rests on top of the unit, and a long, segmented antenna extends vertically from the side. The sides of the device show reinforced corners and additional connection ports, likely for power and auxiliary equipment. The overall design is robust, with visible screws and industrial labels, indicating durability for field or vehicular use. The object is compact enough to be portable, yet substantial in build, suggesting it is designed for reliable communication in demanding urban or outdoor environments.
description: A rugged, olive-green radio transceiver with a digital control panel, coiled handset, and a long antenna, built for durable field use. The unit features a prominent heat sink and reinforced metal housing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the digital display, keypad, control buttons, and the coiled handset attached to the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the antenna, the handset resting on top, and the reinforced metal casing with visible connection ports.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the black heat sink, the top-mounted handset, and the overall boxy structure of the device.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the coiled cord, additional connection ports, and the robust construction of the casing.
